stem: A energy power grid uses 3 parallel generators for redundancy. The power maintained requires at least one generator to function. generator 1 has a reliability of 91%. generator 2 has a reliability of 81%. generator 3 has a reliability between 79% and 96% (uncertain due to limited operational data). What is the overall probability that the power maintained?

ground_truth.answer: P(power maintained) ranges from 0.9964 to 0.9993. Point estimate: 0.9979.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 generators fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.09 × 0.19 × 0.21 = 0.0036. P(works) = 0.9964.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.09 × 0.19 × 0.04 = 0.0007. P(works) = 0.9993.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
